briefdescription.para: uri.c: set of generic URI related routines

detaileddescription.para: Reference: RFCs 3986, 2732 and 2373
See Copyright for the status of this software.
Author: Daniel Veillard

briefdescription.para: The definition of the URI regexp in the above RFC has no size limit In practice they are usually relatively short except for the data URI scheme as defined in RFC 2397.

detaileddescription.para: Even for data URI the usual maximum size before hitting random practical limits is around 64 KB and 4KB is usually a maximum admitted limit for proper operations. The value below is more a security limit than anything else and really should never be hit by 'normal' operations Set to 1 MByte in 2012, this is only enforced on output

briefdescription.para: Make sure the xmlURI struct is free of content.
Parse an URI scheme.

detaileddescription.para: ALPHA *( ALPHA / DIGIT / "+" / "-" / "." )

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the string to analyze

briefdescription.para: Parse the query part of an URI.

detaileddescription.para: fragment = *( pchar / "/" / "?" ) NOTE: the strict syntax as defined by 3986 does not allow '[' and ']' in the fragment identifier but this is used very broadly for xpointer scheme selection, so we are allowing it here to not break for example all the DocBook processing chains.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the string to analyze

briefdescription.para: Parse the query part of an URI.

detaileddescription.para: query = *uric

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the string to analyze

briefdescription.para: Parse a port part and fills in the appropriate fields of the uri structure.

detaileddescription.para: port = *DIGIT

briefdescription.para: Parse an user information part and fills in the appropriate fields of the uri structure.

detaileddescription.para: userinfo = *( unreserved / pct-encoded / sub-delims / ":" )
Skip a dec-octet.

briefdescription.para: Parse an host part and fills in the appropriate fields of the uri structure.

detaileddescription.para: host = IP-literal / IPv4address / reg-name IP-literal = "[" ( IPv6address / IPvFuture ) "]" IPv4address = dec-octet "." dec-octet "." dec-octet "." dec-octet reg-name = *( unreserved / pct-encoded / sub-delims )

briefdescription.para: Parse an authority part and fills in the appropriate fields of the uri structure.

detaileddescription.para: authority = [ userinfo "@" ] host [ ":" port ]

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlURIPtr uri, const char **str, char forbid, int empty)
memberdef.name: xmlParse3986Segment

briefdescription.para: Parse a segment and fills in the appropriate fields of the uri structure.

detaileddescription.para: segment = pchar segment-nz = 1*pchar segment-nz-nc = 1( unreserved / pct-encoded / sub-delims / "@" ) ; non-zero-length segment without any colon ":"

briefdescription.para: Parse an path absolute or empty and fills in the appropriate fields of the uri structure.

detaileddescription.para: path-abempty = *( "/" segment )

briefdescription.para: Parse an path absolute and fills in the appropriate fields of the uri structure.

detaileddescription.para: path-absolute = "/" [ segment-nz *( "/" segment ) ]

briefdescription.para: Parse an path without root and fills in the appropriate fields of the uri structure.

detaileddescription.para: path-rootless = segment-nz *( "/" segment )

briefdescription.para: Parse an path which is not a scheme and fills in the appropriate fields of the uri structure.

detaileddescription.para: path-noscheme = segment-nz-nc *( "/" segment )

briefdescription.para: Parse an hierarchical part and fills in the appropriate fields of the uri structure.

detaileddescription.para: hier-part = "//" authority path-abempty / path-absolute / path-rootless / path-empty

briefdescription.para: Parse an URI string and fills in the appropriate fields of the uri structure.

detaileddescription.para: relative-ref = relative-part [ "?" query ] [ "\#" fragment ] relative-part = "//" authority path-abempty / path-absolute / path-noscheme / path-empty

briefdescription.para: Parse an URI string and fills in the appropriate fields of the uri structure.

detaileddescription.para: scheme ":" hier-part [ "?" query ] [ "\#" fragment ]

briefdescription.para: Parse an URI reference string and fills in the appropriate fields of the uri structure.

detaileddescription.para: URI-reference = URI / relative-ref

briefdescription.para: Parse an URI based on RFC 3986.

detaileddescription.para: URI-reference = [ absoluteURI | relativeURI ] [ "\#" fragment ]

simplesect.para: 0 on success, an error code (typically 1) if the URI is invalid or -1 if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Parse an URI based on RFC 3986.

detaileddescription.para: URI-reference = [ absoluteURI | relativeURI ] [ "\#" fragment ]

simplesect.para: a newly built xmlURI or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: Parse an URI reference string based on RFC 3986 and fills in the appropriate fields of the uri structure.

detaileddescription.para: URI-reference = URI / relative-ref

briefdescription.para: Parse an URI but allows to keep intact the original fragments.

detaileddescription.para: URI-reference = URI / relative-ref

parameterdescription.para: if 1 unescaping of URI pieces are disabled

simplesect.para: a newly built xmlURI or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: Simply creates an empty xmlURI.

simplesect.para: the new structure or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: Function to handle properly a reallocation when saving an URI Also imposes some limit on the length of an URI string output.
Save the URI as an escaped string.

simplesect.para: a new string (to be deallocated by caller)

briefdescription.para: Prints the URI in the stream stream.
Free up the xmlURI struct.
Normalize a filesystem path or URI.

parameterdescription.para: true for filesystem paths, false for URIs

briefdescription.para: Applies the 5 normalization steps to a path stringthat is, RFC 2396 Section 5.2, steps 6.c through 6.g.

detaileddescription.para: Normalization occurs directly on the string, no new allocation is done

briefdescription.para: Unescaping routine, but does not check that the string is an URI.

detaileddescription.para: The output is a direct unsigned char translation of XX values (no encoding) Note that the length of the result can only be smaller or same size as the input string.

parameterdescription.para: the length in bytes to unescape (or <= 0 to indicate full string)

simplesect.para: a copy of the string, but unescaped, will return NULL only in case of error

briefdescription.para: This routine escapes a string to hex, ignoring unreserved characters a-z, A-Z, 0-9, "-._~", a few sub-delims "!*'()", the gen-delim "@" (why?) and the characters in the exception list.

parameterdescription.para: exception list string of chars not to escape

simplesect.para: a new escaped string or NULL in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Escaping routine, does not do validity checks ! It will try to escape the chars needing this, but this is heuristic based it's impossible to be sure.

detaileddescription.para:
25 May 2001 Uses xmlParseURI and xmlURIEscapeStr to try to escape correctly according to RFC2396.
Carl Douglas
str
the string of the URI to escape
an copy of the string, but escaped

briefdescription.para: Resolves a filesystem path from a base path.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, -1 if a memory allocation failed or an error code if URI or base are invalid.

briefdescription.para: Computes he final URI of the reference done by checking that the given URI is valid, and building the final URI using the base URI.

detaileddescription.para: This is processed according to section 5.2 of the RFC 2396
5.2. Resolving Relative References to Absolute Form

parameterdescription.para: the URI instance found in the document

simplesect.para: 0 on success, -1 if a memory allocation failed or an error code if URI or base are invalid.

briefdescription.para: Computes he final URI of the reference done by checking that the given URI is valid, and building the final URI using the base URI.

detaileddescription.para: This is processed according to section 5.2 of the RFC 2396
5.2. Resolving Relative References to Absolute Form

parameterdescription.para: the URI instance found in the document

simplesect.para: a new URI string (to be freed by the caller) or NULL in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Expresses the URI of the reference in terms relative to the base.

detaileddescription.para:
Some examples of this operation include: base = "http://site1.com/docs/book1.html" URI input URI returned http://site1.com/docs/pic1.gif pic1.gif http://site2.com/docs/pic1.gif http://site2.com/docs/pic1.gif
base = "docs/book1.html" URI input URI returned docs/pic1.gif pic1.gif docs/img/pic1.gif img/pic1.gif img/pic1.gif ../img/pic1.gif http://site1.com/docs/pic1.gif http://site1.com/docs/pic1.gif
2.13.0
URI
the URI reference under consideration
base
the base value
valPtr
pointer to result URI
0 on success, -1 if a memory allocation failed or an error code if URI or base are invalid.

briefdescription.para: See xmlBuildRelativeURISafe.

simplesect.para: a new URI string (to be freed by the caller) or NULL in case error.

briefdescription.para: Prepares a path.

detaileddescription.para: If the path contains the substring "://", it is considered a Legacy Extended IRI. Characters which aren't allowed in URIs are escaped.
Otherwise, the path is considered a filesystem path which is copied without modification.
The caller is responsible for freeing the memory occupied by the returned string. If there is insufficient memory available, or the argument is NULL, the function returns NULL.

parameterdescription.para: the resource locator in a filesystem notation

briefdescription.para: Constructs an URI expressing the existing path.

parameterdescription.para: the resource locator in a filesystem notation

simplesect.para: a new URI, or a duplicate of the path parameter if the construction fails. The caller is responsible for freeing the memory occupied by the returned string. If there is insufficient memory available, or the argument is NULL, the function returns NULL.
